topic: Someone discovers their neighbor is secretly wealthy
raw_output:
[story 1]

The pristine white of Mr. Henderson’s new landscaping always grated on my nerves. Every perfectly manicured hedge, every impossibly vibrant rose bush, felt like a deliberate affront to the slightly overgrown, decidedly less polished yard I maintained. I watched from behind my dusty window, a tight knot forming in my chest. He was a titan, I supposed, judging by the sleek, silent black sedan that pulled up every Tuesday, its driver emerging with an air of effortless entitlement. I made sure my own gardening tools were left just slightly askew, a small, deliberate imperfection aimed squarely at his flawless facade. When he waved—a casual, dismissive gesture—I offered a curt nod, my jaw set tight enough to ache.

[story 2]

I kept catching myself staring at the gleaming chrome of the new gate. It was absurd. Mr. Henderson, who struggled to pay the electric bill last month, now had a fortress of polished steel surrounding his property. A slow, simmering resentment began to bubble up inside me, hot and acidic. I started timing my walks past his property line, making sure my footsteps sounded heavy and deliberate, just to remind him that I was *here*, observing his excessive display. One afternoon, I deliberately dropped a small, harmless pebble near the edge of his walkway. I watched his housekeeper rush out, her face a mask of practiced neutrality, and I felt a strange, satisfying tightening in my gut.

[story 3]

It was the sheer, unearned ease with which he navigated the neighborhood that truly set me off. He never seemed to worry about anything—no complaints about the noisy construction next door, no concern over the rising property taxes. It just *was*. I found myself cataloging his possessions from my vantage point: the expensive, silent security system, the automatic sprinkler system that seemed to run on its own budget. A sharp, almost painful feeling flared in my throat when I saw him briefly interacting with a delivery driver; the way he didn't even need to speak, just a slight inclination of his head, was enough to make my knuckles whiten where I gripped my coffee mug. I made a point of lingering by my own fence, just to be in his line of sight, letting the silence between us speak volumes.
